_note: Orchestrator-level run knobs (cross-cutting). Loaded once per run by src/methods/experimental.py (stage 9). The `server.{uvicorn,waitress,gunicorn}` blocks own the per-spawner runtime tuning (backlog, threads/workers, readiness + shutdown grace windows); the spawners read them via src/experimental/prototype/runtime/config.py. The `trial` block carries the load-generation knobs (n_requests, request_rate_per_s, consumer_pool_size, max_queue_depth, drain_timeout_s, kind_probability, atomic_response_time_overrides) used by the open-loop trial driver.

trial._note_rate: request_rate_per_s = 248 matches `lambda_z` = TAS_{1}.lambda_z in `data/config/profile/dflt.json` (the case-study design point). The latest calibration on this host measured a per-worker saturation knee at ~328 req/s, so the envelopes under `data/results/calibration/<dpl>/` were manually bumped to `r_max_req_s = 350` to keep the bounds gate aligned with the case-study design rather than the host's hardware ceiling. The undershoot (real throughput < 248) lands in `verdict.operational.X_0_req_per_s` for stage 9 to see.
trial._note_open_loop: The trial driver is open-loop (producer/consumer over an asyncio.Queue): a dispatcher pushes ticks at request_rate_per_s regardless of how fast the server replies; consumer_pool_size consumers drain the queue, each running its own User + httpx pool. The in-flight ceiling equals consumer_pool_size (one in-flight per consumer at a time), so keep it below httpx's max_connections=100 default to avoid pool throttling. When the server saturates, the queue grows; on hitting max_queue_depth the dispatcher blocks at queue.put() so saturation surfaces as an offered-rate undershoot in verdict.operational.X_0_req_per_s. drain_timeout_s caps the wait for in-flight consumers at trial end.
